summary: Drew DeVault's March 2022 status update covers his work on a kernel for his programming language (Hare), including interrupt handling and backtraces, as well as a PNG decoder that demonstrates the language's strengths. He also briefly covers SourceHut's migration to Alpine 3.15 and ongoing GraphQL improvements.
key_points: A kernel is being written in Hare, with working interrupt handlers and backtrace support
A PNG decoder showcasing Hare's strengths passes 77 of 161 test vectors, with clean memory management and I/O abstractions
SourceHut completed migration to Alpine 3.15 and landed GraphQL refactorings
Plans to shift focus back from the language to SourceHut in the coming month
money_quotes: This library is a good demonstration of the strengths of the language.

It has simple code which presents a comprehensive interface for the file format, has a strong user-directed memory management model, takes good advantage of features like slices, and makes good use of standard library features like compress::zlib and the I/O abstraction.
key_insight: Hare is maturing enough to write both a kernel and a PNG decoder that showcase its design strengths in memory management and I/O abstractions.
